overview.Title: Phishing-Based Malware Deployment Against Water Infrastructure
overview.Summary: A basic-level cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems through a phishing campaign followed by malware deployment to disrupt operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure, potentially linked to geopolitical conflicts.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: Targets mobile users with phishing attacks to solicit sensitive info.
stage.description: The attack begins with a phishing email containing a malicious link, designed to trick employees into clicking and revealing credentials or downloading malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 445 Name: Malicious Logic Insertion into Product Software via Configuration Management Manipulation Description: Exploits a configuration management system to insert malicious logic into software builds.
stage.description: After gaining initial access via phishing, the attacker deploys malware (e.g., CaddyWiper) to disrupt operations by targeting SCADA systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 673 Name: Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software Description: Malicious code is inserted into legitimate software, signed by the developer unknowingly.
stage.description: The attacker creates a backdoor to maintain persistent access, ensuring they can re-enter the system later to escalate privileges or deploy further attacks.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement Description: Replaces legitimate hardware with counterfeit components to cause disruption or allow further compromise.
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in the SCADA system to gain higher privileges, enabling direct control over industrial processes.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 456 Name: Infected Memory Description: Injects malicious logic into memory to evade detection and achieve long-term presence.
stage.description: By disabling antivirus software, the attacker ensures their malware remains undetected, allowing continued manipulation of the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement Description: Replaces legitimate hardware with counterfeit components to cause disruption or allow further compromise.
stage.description: The attacker modifies control parameters within the SCADA system, leading to operational disruptions such as pump failures or unintended valve openings.

Scenario.summary: The attack starts with a phishing email targeting employees, leading to malware deployment and subsequent system compromises. Through persistence and privilege escalation, the attacker gains control over critical infrastructure, resulting in significant operational impacts.
